source.book: 《9分达人雅思听力真题还原及解析7》

segments.text: Welcome to our lovely tour around Hobson Park.
segments.translation: 欢迎参加我们美妙的霍布森公园之旅。

segments.text: Visible from much of Auckland, this is a widely recognised local landmark.
segments.translation: 从奥克兰的许多地方都能看到这里，它是当地广为人知的地标。

segments.text: I hope you'll enjoy your stay here.
segments.translation: 希望您在这里游览愉快。

segments.text: But first, let me briefly introduce its history to you before we stroll around the park.
segments.translation: 不过，在我们漫步公园之前，先让我为大家简要介绍一下它的历史。

segments.text: Hobson Park is located on an island that used to be a volcano, which emerged just 600 years ago.
segments.translation: 霍布森公园坐落在一座岛上，这座岛原本是一座火山，直到600年前才形成。

segments.text: You can still enjoy the breathtaking views of a nearby lake today.
segments.translation: 如今您仍然可以欣赏附近湖泊令人叹为观止的景色。

segments.text: The lava flow at the end of Takapuna Beach enveloped a kauri forest.
segments.translation: 塔卡普纳海滩尽头的熔岩流掩埋了一片贝壳杉林。

segments.text: It is known to be New Zealand's only example of a fossil forest preserved in a lava flow.
segments.translation: 据悉，这是新西兰唯一一处保存在熔岩流中的森林化石遗迹。

segments.text: Actually, Hobson Park was named after William Hobson, a British captain and also the first governor of New Zealand.
segments.translation: 事实上，霍布森公园是以英国海军上校、新西兰首任总督威廉·霍布森的名字命名的。

segments.text: At the time, the British were making plans to claim New Zealand as a colony to ensure their trade interest.
segments.translation: 当时，英国人正计划将新西兰据为殖民地，以保障其贸易利益。

segments.text: War broke out between Maori troops and government forces.
segments.translation: 毛利军队与政府军之间爆发了战争。

segments.text: After years of revolt, two Maori chiefs were invited to the island to restore peace.
segments.translation: 经过多年的反抗，两位毛利酋长受邀来到岛上恢复和平。

segments.text: They signed a treaty and shook hands with Governor Hobson.
segments.translation: 他们签署了一项条约，并与霍布森总督握手。

segments.text: While local tribes started to celebrate their success, the two Maori chiefs didn't stop promoting Maori self-determination in meetings and correspondence with the government.
segments.translation: 当地部落开始庆祝胜利之际，这两位毛利酋长并未停止在会议中以及与政府的通信中推动毛利人自治。

segments.text: In 1840, Hobson chose Auckland as the site for his capital.
segments.translation: 1840年，霍布森选择奥克兰作为首都所在地。

segments.text: The same year, plans for founding Hobson Park were initiated.
segments.translation: 同年，建立霍布森公园的计划正式启动。

segments.text: But it was in 1842 that the park was actually created and officially opened to the public.
segments.translation: 但公园实际上是在1842年才建成并正式向公众开放。

segments.text: As you can see, the park is immense.
segments.translation: 正如大家所见，这座公园面积广阔。

segments.text: It is comprised of many natural wonders as well as artificial features.
segments.translation: 园内既有许多自然奇观，也有人工设施。

segments.text: Just a few hundred metres away, there is a duck pond where you can observe the waterbirds, including some rare breeds.
segments.translation: 就在几百米外有一个鸭塘，您可以在那里观察水鸟，其中包括一些稀有品种。

segments.text: But remember to keep a distance from them and refrain from feeding them.
segments.translation: 不过请记得与它们保持距离，也不要给它们喂食。

segments.text: Across the iron bridge, you'll get to formal gardens used for experiments on breeding local wild species.
segments.translation: 穿过铁桥，您会到达几座规则式花园，那里用于开展本地野生物种的繁育实验。

segments.text: If you keep roaming down the trail, you'll see the old residence of Maori people forming a small village.
segments.translation: 如果沿着小径继续漫步，您会看到毛利人的旧居，它们组成了一个小村庄。

segments.text: These houses are full of local paintings, sculptures and artefacts, all donated by the city council to showcase the history and culture of Maori people living there.
segments.translation: 这些房屋里陈列着当地的绘画、雕塑和手工艺品，全部由市议会捐赠，用于展示居住于此的毛利人的历史与文化。

segments.text: And these are not something you'll stumble upon in a regular museum.
segments.translation: 这些可不是在普通博物馆里随随便便就能见到的东西。

segments.text: Oh, and don't forget to visit the Temperate House.
segments.translation: 哦，别忘了参观温带植物馆。

segments.text: It's only a ten-minute walk away from the duck pond.
segments.translation: 从鸭塘步行过去只需十分钟。

segments.text: It accommodates a whole range of wild plants from around the country.
segments.translation: 馆内汇集了来自全国各地的各种野生植物。

segments.text: But what makes it unique is the forest of kauri trees, a native species to New Zealand.
segments.translation: 但它的独特之处在于那片贝壳杉林，贝壳杉是新西兰的本土树种。

segments.text: However, the kauri tree population was threatened by the spread of kauri dieback disease and is now under the protection of the Department of Conservation.
segments.translation: 然而，贝壳杉种群曾受到贝壳杉枯死病蔓延的威胁，目前由自然保护部负责保护。

segments.text: Let me point out the whereabouts of some other critical features in the park in case you need them.
segments.translation: 我来指出公园里其他一些重要设施的位置，以备大家需要。

segments.text: If you are a plant lover and want to take some local greenery home, you may wish to visit the plant nursery, where you can choose from an extensive collection of plants from succulents to some rare local species.
segments.translation: 如果您喜爱植物，想带些本地绿植回家，不妨去苗圃看看，那里有丰富的植物可供挑选，从多肉植物到一些珍稀本地物种都有。

segments.text: It's right next to the museum, on the same side of the road.
segments.translation: 苗圃就在博物馆旁边，位于道路同一侧。

segments.text: There is a pine tree hill with tracks for hikers of all abilities.
segments.translation: 园内有一座松树山，山上的步道适合各种水平的徒步者。

segments.text: The whole trip takes about half an hour.
segments.translation: 走完全程大约需要半小时。

segments.text: There is a steep climb in places.
segments.translation: 部分路段坡度很陡。

segments.text: Tread carefully and stick to the formal tracks.
segments.translation: 请小心行走，并始终走正规的步道。

segments.text: You'll be rewarded with great views.
segments.translation: 您将欣赏到绝佳的景色。

segments.text: If you leave your car in the car park, you can see it just across the road towards George Street entry.
segments.translation: 如果您把车停在停车场，从停车场过马路、朝乔治街入口方向就能看到它。

segments.text: Or if you are an experienced hiker and want some challenge, you can choose Eagle Mountain.
segments.translation: 或者，如果您经验丰富又想接受一些挑战，可以选择鹰山。

segments.text: It offers fantastic hiking routes.
segments.translation: 那里有很棒的徒步路线。

segments.text: It's also an ideal spot for bird watching, with many wild species unique to New Zealand.
segments.translation: 那里也是观鸟的理想地点，有许多新西兰特有的野生鸟类。

segments.text: We are right here, at the Wellington entrance.
segments.translation: 我们现在就在这里，也就是惠灵顿入口。

segments.text: Then take the 1st right, and you'll see Eagle Mountain immediately to your right.
segments.translation: 然后在第一个路口右转，您马上就会在右手边看到鹰山。

segments.text: If you're hungry and want to take a quick bite, there is a nearby food kiosk providing snacks and beverages.
segments.translation: 如果您饿了，想简单吃点东西，附近有一家提供小吃和饮料的食品亭。

segments.text: Start from the Wellington entrance, take the 1st left, keep walking down the road, and after the junction, where it meets a road connecting the George Street entry, you'll see it on your right.
segments.translation: 从惠灵顿入口出发，在第一个路口左转，沿路一直走，经过与通往乔治街入口的道路相交的路口后，您就会在右手边看到它。

segments.text: And lastly, there is a conservation area protecting kauri trees just by the Winter Garden.
segments.translation: 最后，冬季花园旁边有一片保护贝壳杉的自然保护区。

segments.text: Unfortunately, it's currently out of bounds to the public.
segments.translation: 遗憾的是，目前公众不得进入。

segments.text: Ok, now, if you have anything else to ask, I'm here to help ...
segments.translation: 好了，现在如果大家还有其他问题，我很乐意解答……
